condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I began by querying my research tool for TAC Protocol's current price, the daily closing prices for July 11–13, 2026, the 7-day low, and recent volatility. My first `research` call errored (I passed a bare `query` field instead of the required `questions` array), but I self-corrected immediately. The nested research agent pulled CoinGecko's historical data page and cross-checked CoinMarketCap, Crypto.com, CoinStats, CoinCarp and CryptoRank.

## Key Evidence

- **Known in-window closes (CoinGecko historical):** July 11 = **$0.00370137**, July 12 = **$0.00299534** — both comfortably **above** the $0.0020 threshold, so no early/pre-publication resolution.
- **Live price:** July 13 ≈ **$0.00264** (day not yet finalized). The threshold is only **~24% below** this level.
- **Trajectory:** A **~90%+ flash crash on July 7, 2026** (futures liquidation cascade + 18-wallet dump) from the June 30 ATH of $0.06715. Post-crash bounce to $0.00471 (Jul 9), then a stepwise decline: $0.00392 → $0.00370 → $0.00300 → ~$0.00264.
- **Downtrend:** every daily close since the July 9 bounce fell, averaging roughly **-13%/day**.
- **Proximity:** the 7-day intraday low was already **$0.002456**, very close to $0.0020.
- **Volatility:** extreme close-to-close moves (+72% / -17% / -6% / -19% / -12%), intraday July 13 range $0.002617–$0.003226.

## Modeling

I ran a Monte Carlo (100K paths, 11 remaining daily closes from $0.00264) across four weighted drift/vol scenarios, deliberately excluding the +72% crash-recovery outlier so the drift wasn't contaminated by the crash regime:
- Continued downtrend (μ=-0.08, σ=0.15, w=0.30) → 0.937
- Mild drift down (μ=-0.03, σ=0.15, w=0.30) → 0.693
- Stabilize/no drift (μ=0.00, σ=0.15, w=0.20) → 0.468
- Wider-vol downtrend (μ=-0.06, σ=0.22, w=0.20) → 0.837

Weighted **P(YES) ≈ 0.75**. My no-drift anchor alone gives ~0.47.

## Conclusion

I ran a reflection (four factors: strong downtrend, proximity to threshold, many touch opportunities, offset by a support/dead-cat NO factor) and a premortem stress-test (support band at $0.0020–$0.0025 may hold; the daily-CLOSE requirement is a genuine hurdle since intraday dips don't count; a single bounce day resets the buffer). Discounting slightly below the raw 0.75 simulation for support/bounce risk, I concluded **~0.72 (logit 0.94)**.

capability_gaps: No direct CoinGecko API / structured OHLC time-series tool — the agent had to scrape the historical_data HTML page, which only exposed closes (no daily high/low), limiting intraday-breach reasoning.
subtle_bugs: fetch_url on coinmarketcap.com/currencies/tac-protocol/historical-data/ returned only navigation-menu text (no data table), silently yielding no historical data; the agent correctly fell back to CoinGecko rather than being misled.

classification.approach: Weighted Monte Carlo random-walk simulation from the current live price over 11 remaining daily closes, anchored on CoinGecko daily-close data.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether the post-crash downtrend continues into at least one close ≤$0.0020 or whether support in the $0.0020–$0.0025 band holds, given the daily-CLOSE (not intraday-low) requirement.

risk_flags.evidence: The four-scenario weighting is subjective and tilts bearish (60% weight on down-drift scenarios). The 'stabilize/no drift' scenario alone gives only 0.468, and July 13 was actually showing intraday recovery (+18.6% per CoinGecko). A more neutral weighting toward the no-drift anchor could pull the estimate toward the low-60s; the ~0.72 leans on continuation of a very short (4-day) trend.

No prediction-market check (Polymarket/Kalshi/Manifold) and no independent daily-close volatility calibration beyond the 4 recent post-bounce returns; the σ=0.15–0.22 inputs are hand-set rather than estimated from a longer close-to-close series.

strengths: Correctly identified that the two already-known in-window closes (Jul 11 $0.00370, Jul 12 $0.00300) are above threshold, so no early resolution — and built a forward-looking model from the live ~$0.00264 rather than the stale $0.0038 implied by the question.
Deliberately excluded the +72% crash-recovery outlier from the drift estimate so the Monte Carlo wasn't contaminated by the crash regime.
Explicitly engaged the daily-CLOSE (not intraday-low) resolution criterion in both the reflection and premortem, treating the intraday-vs-close distinction as a genuine hurdle.
Ran a multi-scenario weighted Monte Carlo (100K paths each) rather than a single-point random walk, and reported a no-drift anchor (~0.47) to contextualize the upward shift.
Cross-checked the live price across 6+ trackers and correctly reconciled the CoinGecko +18.6% vs CMC/Crypto.com -17% discrepancy as a 24h-window artifact.
weaknesses: The scenario weights (30/30/20/20, bearish-tilted) are asserted without justification; the final probability is quite sensitive to them and no sensitivity analysis was shown.
Extrapolated a ~-13%/day drift from only 4 post-bounce daily returns — a very short sample for anchoring an 11-day forward simulation.

Did not reconcile the modeling choice of starting from $0.00264 as the 'live' price while July 13's own close is itself still uncertain and could already breach — the simulation treats day 1 as the first uncertain close but the buffer above $0.0020 is thin.
Tool-audit note claimed 'No failures' despite the initial research validation error (self-corrected), a trivial understatement flagged even by the premortem.

issues: Initial mcp__research__research call failed a pydantic validation (query vs questions field) — self-corrected, minimal token waste.
Multiple ToolSearch calls returned empty results, adding minor overhead in the nested research agent.
CoinMarketCap historical-data fetch returned only nav content, providing no usable data.
